title: feature: named databases with --db flag

title: transform: Local OCR backend (RapidOCR)
description:
Make the OCR backend pluggable so quarry can run without AWS credentials.

## Motivation
- Eliminates AWS dependency and per-page cost ($1.50/1000 pages)
- Enables offline use
- Simpler setup (no IAM, S3 bucket, or credentials needed)

## Approach
- Define an OcrBackend protocol with two operations: sync single-page OCR and async multi-page OCR
- Implement TextractBackend (existing code in ocr_client.py)
- Implement LocalBackend wrapping a local engine (PaddleOCR, EasyOCR, or Tesseract)
- Add OCR_BACKEND setting (default: "textract", alternative: "local")
- Pipeline calls backend through protocol, no other changes needed

## Candidates
- PaddleOCR: best accuracy, ~100MB models, CPU or GPU
- EasyOCR: good accuracy, PyTorch-based, ~200MB models
- Tesseract: simplest install, weakest accuracy on noisy/skewed scans

## Open Questions
- Which local engine? PaddleOCR has best benchmarks but adds PaddlePaddle dependency
- Should local be an optional extra (quarry-mcp[local])?
- Acceptable accuracy threshold for handwritten/skewed documents?
close_reason: Implemented LocalOcrBackend using RapidOCR (pure Python, ONNX Runtime). Default changed from textract to local. 6 commits on feat/local-ocr branch.

title: MCP side-effect tools should fire-and-forget per cli.md standard
description:
Six MCP tools block synchronously but are side-effects where the caller doesn't need the return value to proceed. Per punt-kit/standards/cli.md §Sync vs Async, these should use the fire-and-forget pattern (threading.Thread + daemon=True, return optimistic result immediately).

Non-compliant tools:
1. `ingest` — file/URL ingestion, can be slow (network + embedding)
2. `remember` — inline content ingestion, can be slow (embedding)
3. `delete` — collection/document deletion
4. `register_directory` — directory registration for sync
5. `deregister_directory` — directory deregistration
6. `sync_all_registrations` — syncs all registered directories (most critical — can take minutes)

Pattern to follow: vox/src/punt_vox/server.py (unmute/record tools).

Implementation approach:
- Add `_predict_result()` helpers that return optimistic success responses
- Wrap each blocking call in `threading.Thread(target=..., daemon=True).start()`
- Return the predicted result immediately
- Document eventual consistency: ingest → immediate search may miss new docs

Reference: punt-kit/standards/cli.md §Sync vs Async, §MCP fire-and-forget pattern
close_reason: Merged PR #81: fire-and-forget MCP tools with bounded ThreadPoolExecutor

title: Add WebSocket /mcp endpoint for mcp-proxy
description:
## Why

mcp-proxy is a Go binary that bridges MCP stdio transport to shared daemon processes over WebSocket. Currently, each Claude Code session spawns a separate quarry MCP server process (~200MB memory each). With mcp-proxy, all sessions share one quarry daemon — mcp-proxy handles the stdio side and connects to the daemon over WebSocket. Quarry currently has an HTTP server (`quarry serve`) but no WebSocket endpoint for mcp-proxy to connect to.

## What

Add a WebSocket upgrade handler at `/mcp` on the existing `quarry serve` HTTP server.

The handler should:

- Accept WebSocket upgrade with `session_key` query parameter (e.g., `ws://localhost:8080/mcp?session_key=12345`)
- Read MCP JSON-RPC text messages from the WebSocket (each message is a separate WebSocket text frame — no newline delimiter needed, unlike stdio which uses newline-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0)
- Route incoming messages through the existing MCP tool handler (same code path as the current stdio MCP server)
- Write JSON-RPC responses back as WebSocket text messages
- Support multiple concurrent connections (one per Claude Code session)

## Technical Notes

- Check what HTTP framework `quarry serve` uses (likely Starlette/Uvicorn or similar) — use its native WebSocket support rather than adding a separate library
- Session key identifies which Claude Code session the connection belongs to. Passed as `?session_key=PID` query parameter. For now, just log it and associate it with the connection for debugging — no auth or routing logic needed yet
- Request-response only for quarry (no server-initiated push needed yet, unlike biff which needs bidirectional messaging)
- The MCP tool handler should be factored so both stdio and WebSocket code paths use the same handler function — no duplication of tool dispatch logic
- Connection lifecycle: WebSocket open → exchange messages → client closes (mcp-proxy exits) or server shuts down. Standard WebSocket close handshake

## Testing

- Unit tests: WebSocket handler accepts upgrade, reads a JSON-RPC request, returns correct response
- Integration test infrastructure exists in mcp-proxy at `internal/integration/quarry_test.go` (placeholder with `integration` build tag) — that test will exercise the full path: stdin → mcp-proxy → WebSocket → quarry → response back
- Test with `websocat` or similar tool for manual verification: `websocat ws://localhost:8080/mcp?session_key=test`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: nothing
- Blocks: mcp-proxy slice 4 (quarry integration test — the first real end-to-end test of the proxy)
close_reason: Merged PR #95: WebSocket /mcp endpoint + Starlette migration

title: CLI global flags: reference implementation (compliant)
description:
## Status: FULLY COMPLIANT

Quarry CLI passes all 5 global flag checks from punt-kit/standards/cli.md.

### What makes it reference-quality

1. **Flag ordering** — `--json`, `--verbose/-v`, `--quiet/-q`, `--db` (project-specific) in correct order (`src/quarry/__main__.py:67-86`)
2. **invoke_without_command=True** — Set on callback with help block (lines 64, 97-99)
3. **No argv hoisting** — Zero `sys.argv` manipulation
4. **Centralized `_emit()` adapter** — All 24 commands use `_emit(data, text)` exclusively. Zero scattered `if json_output` checks outside the adapter (lines 102-113). The `_progress()` helper appropriately suppresses progress bars in JSON mode.
5. **Mutual exclusion** — Validated at lines 90-92 with test coverage at `tests/test_cli.py:1410-1413`

### Pattern: Direct Delegation with _emit()

```
CLI command  ──▶  core.function()  ──▶  _emit(json_data, text)
                                            │
                                      _emit() handles:
                                      - JSON vs text branching
                                      - stdout routing
```

This is the recommended pattern for synchronous direct-delegation CLIs. Simpler than biff's _run() pattern — appropriate when commands don't need async context management. Other projects adopting the standard should reference this implementation.
close_reason: Already compliant. Documented as reference implementation for other projects.

title: Smart /ingest: auto-discover sitemap from any URL
description:
When /ingest receives a URL like https://code.claude.com/docs, auto-discover the site's sitemap before falling back to single-page ingest.

Discovery steps (in Python, not prompt):
1. Fetch {origin}/robots.txt — parse Sitemap: directives
2. If no sitemap in robots.txt, try {origin}/sitemap.xml directly
3. If sitemap found, call ingest_sitemap with include_patterns from the URL path prefix (e.g. /docs/*)
4. If no sitemap found, fall back to ingest_url for the single page

Implementation: new MCP tool (e.g. ingest_smart or enhance ingest_url) that wraps the discovery logic in pipeline.py. Update /ingest command to call it for URLs instead of ingest_url directly.

title: tool: Hybrid document-level search
description:
Add document-level search combining sparse (keyword) and dense (vector) retrieval to support exhaustive queries like 'how much did I spend on maintenance' where top-N chunk similarity is the wrong tool.

## Problem

Current search returns top-N chunks by vector similarity. Works for conceptual queries but fails for exhaustive queries that need ALL matching documents regardless of similarity rank. Document names are unreliable (e.g. scan.pdf, IMG_5349.jpg) so metadata filtering by name is insufficient.

## Design

### Document metadata table

At ingestion time, after chunking and embedding, generate and store per-document:
- Document-level embedding: np.mean(chunk_vectors, axis=0) — already computed, just store the mean
- Keywords: TF-IDF or statistical extraction from page text — zero additional API cost
- Store in a separate document_metadata table in LanceDB

### New MCP tool: find_documents

Hybrid search against the document metadata table:
- Dense: vector similarity against document-level embedding
- Sparse: keyword/BM25 matching against extracted terms
- LanceDB supports both natively (full-text search index + vector index)
- Returns document-level results (name, collection, pages, keywords) not chunks
- Small responses — document list only, no chunk text

### Retrieval flow for exhaustive queries

1. find_documents(collection='hallberg-rassy', query='invoice') → complete document list (small response)
2. get_page(doc, page) for each document → extract specific data
3. LLM aggregates the answers

### Retrieval flow for conceptual queries

search_documents (existing) — unchanged, top-N chunk similarity

## Cost

Zero additional API calls. Keywords extracted from existing text. Document embedding is mean of existing chunk vectors. Both computed at end of _chunk_embed_store.

title: infra: Add page_type and source_format columns to LanceDB schema
description:
Add two new string columns to the LanceDB chunk schema and populate them during ingestion for all existing formats.

## Schema changes
- `page_type` (pa.utf8()) — content type: text, code, spreadsheet, presentation, email
- `source_format` (pa.utf8()) — file extension or source indicator: .pdf, .py, .xlsx, "inline", etc.

## Pipeline changes
- Pass page_type through from PageContent to Chunk to LanceDB record
- Add source_format (derived from file extension) at ingest time
- For ingest_content (raw text ingestion), set source_format to "inline" to distinguish from file-sourced documents
- Fix document_path — currently hardcoded to "<string>" for inline content; consider a more intentional sentinel or empty string
- Update all existing ingest functions: ingest_pdf, ingest_text_file, ingest_image, ingest_code_file, ingest_text_content

## PageType redefinition
- PDF pages: both TEXT and IMAGE extraction produce page_type="text" in stored chunks
- SECTION → "text" (MD, TXT, DOCX, TEX are all prose)
- CODE stays "code"
- The PageType enum in models.py may need updating or the mapping happens at storage time

## Inline content provenance
- ingest_text (to be renamed ingest_content) currently stores synthetic values: document_path="<string>", page_number=section index, total_pages=section count
- These fields are semantic lies — get_page returns a "page" that is really a markdown heading section
- source_format="inline" makes this provenance explicit and queryable, so downstream tools can distinguish file-sourced vs inline-sourced chunks

## Breaking change
Existing indexes need re-ingestion (quarry sync) to populate new columns. Acceptable — no production users yet.

title: bug: embed_texts() has no batching — OOM crash on large documents
description: quarry sync consumed 66.3 GB resident memory on a 24 GB M2 MacBook Air, causing macOS to kill 120+ system services via Jetsam and effectively crashing the machine. Root cause: OnnxEmbeddingBackend.embed_texts() in embeddings.py processes ALL chunks for a document in a single ONNX inference call with no batching. For a 575-chunk XLSX, this creates batch shape (575, 512). With ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4), up to 4 large documents embed concurrently — 1,187 chunks total, ~15 GB just for attention matrices in a single layer. Fix: add EMBED_BATCH_SIZE=32 batching loop in embed_texts(). With batch 32, attention per layer drops to 402 MB per thread (~1.6 GB for 4 threads). Also consider reducing default workers from 4 to 2. Full analysis in quarry-sync-oom-bug-report.md.
close_reason: Batched embed_texts() at 32 texts, reduced default workers to 2

title: deregister fails to clean up LanceDB chunks (document_name mismatch)

This means the document_name values in the SQLite registry do not match those in LanceDB. The chunks become orphaned. Needs reproduction: register a small directory, sync, deregister, check if chunks remain.
close_reason: Fixed: threaded document_name through code, text, and HTML processors

title: Unified ingest command dispatching on URI scheme
description:
Replace ingest-file, ingest-url, ingest-sitemap (CLI) and ingest_file, ingest_url, ingest_content, ingest_sitemap, ingest_auto (MCP) with a single unified ingest command.

## Design (2026-02-28)

### CLI: quarry ingest <path-or-url>

Dispatch on input:
- File path (relative or absolute) → local file ingestion
- URL without sitemap → single page fetch + ingest
- URL with sitemap (auto-detected) → crawl sitemap + ingest all pages
- stdin / --content flag → inline content ingestion

One command. The tool infers the type from the input. Not ingest-file and ingest-url.

### MCP: single ingest tool

Parameters:
- path (optional) — local file path
- url (optional) — web URL (auto-detects sitemap)
- content (optional) — inline text content
- title (optional) — document title for inline content
- collection (optional) — target collection

Exactly one of path/url/content must be provided. Replaces ingest_file, ingest_url, ingest_content, ingest_sitemap, ingest_auto.

### Migration

- Deprecate old CLI commands (ingest-file, ingest-url, ingest-sitemap) as aliases during transition
- Remove old MCP tools and register single ingest tool
- Update slash command /ingest (already unified in UX, just needs to call new single tool)

Part of the broader tool naming review (quarry-xjq).

title: tool: Optimize MCP response sizes
description:
Large MCP responses (~12.5k tokens) observed filling context quickly.

## Root cause

Exhaustive queries forced through top-N chunk search — addressed by ocr-4xq (hybrid document-level search).

## Residual optimization for search_documents

Even for conceptual queries, chunk-level search responses are larger than necessary:
- Truncate text field in results (e.g. 200 chars with ellipsis) — callers can use get_page for full text
- Drop chunk_index from results (rarely useful to callers)
- Consider default limit=5 instead of 10
- Consider a brief=true flag for compact responses

These are independent of ocr-4xq and worth doing regardless.

title: Cloud embedding backend (SageMaker) for fast batch ingestion
description:
Deploy the same snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5 model to a SageMaker Serverless Inference endpoint for batch ingestion. Local ONNX stays for queries. Same model, same 768-dim vectors, same embedding space — cloud for throughput during sync, local for latency during search.

Motivation: local ONNX embedding sustains ~11 chunks/s (benchmarked). A 10K-file sync takes ~2 hours. Offloading embedding to cloud creates network I/O that parallelizes (same principle as Textract OCR).

Design:
- Add SageMakerEmbeddingBackend implementing EmbeddingBackend protocol
- Configure via EMBEDDING_BACKEND=sagemaker env var (default remains local)
- sync.py uses cloud backend when configured; search/MCP always uses local
- Workers >1 become useful again when embedding is network-bound
- SageMaker Serverless scales to zero when idle (no cost between syncs)

Key constraint: must use identical model weights so vectors are compatible between ingest and query paths.
close_reason: SageMaker embedding backend shipped in PR #39

title: Respect .gitignore and ignore conventions during directory sync
description:
discover_files() in sync.py uses rglob('*') and only skips dotfiles. It indexes venv/, node_modules/, __pycache__/, build/, dist/, and all other gitignored content. This wastes time, disk, and pollutes search results with third-party code.

Fix: teach discover_files() to respect ignore conventions:
1. .gitignore (nested, standard gitignore spec via pathspec library)
2. .quarryignore (project-level, same syntax)
3. Hardcoded defaults: node_modules/, __pycache__/, .venv/, venv/, .tox/, .nox/, .eggs/, *.egg-info/, dist/, build/

Use the pathspec library (already used by Black/pre-commit) for gitignore-compatible pattern matching. The change is isolated to discover_files() — callers don't change.

Acceptance criteria:
- discover_files() skips files matching .gitignore patterns at any level
- discover_files() skips files matching .quarryignore if present
- Hardcoded defaults always apply (even without .gitignore)
- Existing tests pass; new tests cover ignore behavior
- Quality gates green
close_reason: PR #45 merged: .gitignore, .quarryignore, and hardcoded defaults respected during sync

title: Fix plugin dev/prod isolation: add -dev suffix
description:
## Problem

The quarry plugin's `plugin.json` uses `name: "quarry"` in the working tree, which collides with the marketplace prod name. The health check (`plugin-isolation.py`) reports this as a collision.

## Standard

Per [plugins.md §Dev/Prod Isolation](https://github.com/punt-labs/punt-kit/blob/main/standards/plugins.md):
- Working tree must use `name: "<project>-dev"` (e.g. `quarry-dev`)
- Marketplace uses `name: "<project>"` (e.g. `quarry`)
- All commands need `-dev` variants
- Release scripts (`release-plugin.sh`, `restore-dev-plugin.sh`) swap names at tag time

## Required Changes

1. Rename `plugin.json` name from `quarry` to `quarry-dev`
2. Add `-dev.md` variants for all commands
3. Add `scripts/release-plugin.sh` and `scripts/restore-dev-plugin.sh`
4. Verify `punt audit` passes plugin isolation checks
close_reason: Closed

title: epic: Search-by-format — persist content type and source format in chunks
description:
Enable end users to filter semantic searches by document format (e.g. "find spreadsheets with DCF models"). Requires coordinated changes across schema, ingestion pipeline, and search.

## Design Decisions (settled)

### PageType redefined as content type (Option A)
PageType becomes a **content type** classification, not an extraction method. The TEXT/IMAGE distinction for PDF pages (which described extraction routing) is dropped from stored metadata. After extraction, all prose chunks are the same regardless of whether they came from PyMuPDF or Textract.

Stored PageType values:
- `text` — prose documents (PDF, MD, TXT, DOCX, TEX, OCR results)
- `code` — source code (tree-sitter split)
- `spreadsheet` — tabular data (XLSX, CSV)
- `presentation` — slides (PPTX)
- `email` — structured messages (EML/MBOX, future)

The PDF analyzer still uses TEXT/IMAGE internally for pipeline routing; that classification does not propagate to chunks.

### Two new chunk schema columns
1. **`page_type`** (string) — content type from the enum above. Coarse filter: "show me only spreadsheets."
2. **`source_format`** (string) — file extension (`.pdf`, `.py`, `.xlsx`). Precise filter: "show me only Excel files."

Both stored as LanceDB columns, filterable via SQL WHERE clauses in search.

### XLSX stored as LaTeX tabular
Spreadsheet content is serialized as LaTeX `\begin{tabular}` via `pandas.DataFrame.to_latex()`. This reuses the existing LaTeX-aware text_processor for chunking, and LLMs parse LaTeX tables natively.

### Search API
`search_documents` gains optional `page_type` and `source_format` filter parameters, implemented as LanceDB WHERE clauses.

## Constituent work
- Schema: add `page_type` and `source_format` columns to LanceDB schema
- Pipeline: persist both fields during ingestion for all existing formats
- XLSX ingestion: new spreadsheet_processor using pandas + openpyxl, LaTeX output
- Search: expose filter parameters in MCP tool and CLI
- Backfill: existing indexed documents need re-ingestion (quarry sync) to populate new columns
close_reason: All constituent work shipped: schema columns (quarry-53v), XLSX ingestion (quarry-91r), search filters (quarry-mo5), PPTX ingestion (quarry-ebv)

title: format: XLSX/XLS spreadsheet ingestion
description:
Ingest Excel spreadsheets into quarry.

## Design Decisions (settled)

### Serialization: LaTeX tabular
Spreadsheet content is serialized as LaTeX `\begin{tabular}` via `pandas.DataFrame.to_latex()`. This reuses the existing LaTeX-aware text_processor for chunking, and LLMs parse LaTeX tables natively. Same approach applies to CSV.

### Chunking strategy
One LaTeX tabular block per sheet. Large sheets (>1800 chars after LaTeX conversion) get split by row groups with column headers repeated in each chunk.

### Libraries
- `pandas` — already a transitive dependency of sentence-transformers (free)
- `openpyxl` — XLSX backend for pandas (~4 MB, new explicit dependency)

### PageType
Chunks get `page_type="spreadsheet"` and `source_format=".xlsx"` (or `.csv`, `.xls`).

### Scope
- New `spreadsheet_processor.py` module
- Add `.xlsx`, `.xls`, `.csv` to SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS
- Serialize via pandas to_latex(), feed into text_processor for chunking
- Persist page_type and source_format in chunks
- Handle: multiple sheets (chunk per sheet), merged cells (flatten), formulas (value only)
- Depends on schema columns existing (quarry-8si epic)
close_reason: Closed

title: Replace sentence-transformers with direct ONNX inference
description: sentence-transformers pulls in torch (~2.5GB), transformers, scikit-learn, scipy. We already ship onnxruntime for rapidocr. The snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5 model has an ONNX export available. Replace sentence-transformers with: (1) tokenizers for text tokenization, (2) onnxruntime for inference. This eliminates torch, transformers, scikit-learn, scipy, sympy, mpmath, networkx — roughly 2.5GB and ~40 transitive deps from every install. No API change, no behavior change — just a smaller dependency footprint.

title: Long-running sync blocks conversation — add CLI fallback guidance or async support
description:
quarry sync_all_registrations MCP tool blocks the conversation until all collections finish syncing. With 10+ collections and 500+ documents, this can take 10-30 seconds during which the user can't interact.

## Options

### Option A: CLI fallback (works today, zero changes)

Document that `quarry sync` via the Bash tool with `run_in_background: true` runs in background. The user gets notified when done. The /quarry:sync skill or /sync command could use Bash instead of the MCP tool.

### Option B: Async MCP tool

Add a `sync_async` tool that returns immediately with a job ID. The client polls via a `sync_status` tool, or the sync_all_registrations tool accepts an `async: true` parameter.

MCP may support async tool patterns (investigate what's available in the FastMCP framework for long-running operations).

### Option C: Per-collection sync

Instead of syncing all collections at once, sync individual collections via the CLI. Already possible: `quarry sync` syncs all registered directories. A `quarry sync --collection <name>` flag would allow targeted syncing.

## Recommendation

Option A for now — CLI background execution is free. Option B if MCP adds native async tool support. Option C is independently useful.
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: proactive knowledge hint injection via PostToolUse hooks
description:
Z spec enhancement: Quarry should proactively inject relevant knowledge hints into context at key moments.

## Injection Points
1. SessionStart: inject project standards ('This project follows punt-kit standards. Key conventions: ruff, mypy, pytest, CHANGELOG.md under [Unreleased].')
2. PostToolUse Edit|Write: inject file-type-specific hints based on the edited file
   - CHANGELOG.md → Keep a Changelog format reminders
   - *.tex → Z spec conventions (quad~, ZBOOL, bounded sets)
   - DESIGN.md → ADR format reminder
3. PostToolUse Bash: inject workflow hints based on command patterns
   - git commands → session close protocol reminder
   - test commands → coverage expectations

## Implementation
1. Add PostToolUse hooks for Edit|Write and Bash matchers in hooks.json
2. Create hint resolver: maps (tool_name, file_path or command) → quarry search query
3. Search quarry for top-1 relevant result from standards/conventions collections
4. Return as additionalContext (1-3 sentences max — don't flood context)
5. Cache hints per file type to avoid repeated searches

## Design Decisions
- Hints should be SHORT (1-3 sentences) — context is precious
- Hints should be RELEVANT (semantic search, not keyword match)
- Hints should be DEDUPLICATED (don't repeat the same hint in one session)
- Hints should respect quarry.local.md config (add cfgKnowledgeHints toggle)

## Z Spec Reference
docs/claude-code-quarry.tex, InjectKnowledgeHint operation.
Fires in spIdle (SessionStart) and spProcessing (PostToolUse).

title: pipeline: PII detection and redaction
description:
Prevent PII from reaching LLM context windows via ingestion-time detection and retrieval-time redaction. Secure by default (on by default, opt-out).

## Threat Model

Users unknowingly send PII to LLMs through MCP responses. quarry stores invoices, contracts, and personal documents containing names, addresses, emails, phone numbers, and financial account numbers.

## Design

### Ingestion-time detection
- After chunking, run Microsoft Presidio analyzer on each chunk's text
- Store detected PII spans as metadata: entity type, start offset, end offset
- Store as JSON column (pii_spans) on each chunk row in LanceDB
- Also detect on page_raw_text separately (different offsets than chunk text)
- Embeddings computed on full unredacted text — preserves search quality

### Retrieval-time redaction
- Before returning MCP responses, apply stored spans to replace PII with placeholders
- Placeholder style: [PERSON], [EMAIL], [PHONE], [ADDRESS], etc. (Presidio standard)
- Applies to both search_documents results and get_page responses
- Toggle: pii_redaction setting in Settings, True by default

### Audit tool
- New MCP tool: find documents/chunks containing specific PII entity types
- Query stored pii_spans metadata across all chunks
- Example: 'which documents contain PHONE_NUMBER entities'

## Schema Addition

New column on chunks table:
  pii_spans: utf8 (JSON string)
  pii_spans_raw: utf8 (JSON string, for page_raw_text)

Example value:
  [{"type": "PERSON", "start": 42, "end": 55}, {"type": "EMAIL", "start": 120, "end": 148}]

## Dependencies

- Microsoft Presidio as optional dependency: quarry[pii] extra
- Presidio wraps spaCy + regex recognizers, runs locally, no API cost
- PII entity types: PERSON, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PHONE_NUMBER, LOCATION/ADDRESS, CREDIT_CARD, US_SSN, IBAN, and others from Presidio defaults

## Configuration

- pii_redaction: bool = True (secure by default)
- pii_entity_types: list of entity types to detect (default: all Presidio defaults)
- Per-collection config deferred

## Commits (rough)

1. Add Presidio as optional dependency, pii_redaction setting
2. Add pii_spans/pii_spans_raw columns to LanceDB schema
3. PII detection module wrapping Presidio analyzer
4. Integrate detection into _chunk_embed_store pipeline
5. Retrieval-time redaction in search and get_page MCP responses
6. Audit MCP tool: query PII spans across chunks
7. Tests for detection, redaction, and audit

title: Fix [skip ci] in release-plugin.sh blocking tag-triggered workflows

title: chore: Pin embedding model version and reduce HuggingFace network calls
description:
## Problem

Every quarry operation that loads the embedding model makes HTTP requests to HuggingFace Hub to check for updates, even when the model is already cached locally. This adds latency, requires network access, and generates "unauthenticated requests" warnings.

Additionally, the model version is not pinned — `sentence_transformers.SentenceTransformer("Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5")` downloads whatever the latest revision is.

## Questions to investigate

1. Can we pin to a specific git revision/commit hash for reproducibility?
2. Can we set `local_files_only=True` after `quarry install` to skip network checks?
3. Should we vendor the model or use a content-addressed cache?
4. Are there other dependencies making unnecessary network calls at runtime?
close_reason: Shipped in v0.4.1. Pinned revision e58a8f75, local_files_only=True. 4s→0.6s first load.

title: format: PPTX/PPT presentation ingestion
description:
Ingest PowerPoint presentations into quarry.

## Design Decisions (settled)

### Chunking strategy
One slide = one chunk. Slide title + body text + speaker notes concatenated. Natural boundary that matches how presentations are structured.

### PageType
Chunks get `page_type="presentation"` and `source_format=".pptx"`.

### Tables in slides
Tables within slides serialized as LaTeX tabular (same approach as XLSX). Consistent representation for all tabular data regardless of source.

### Embedded images
Phase 1: skip. Phase 2: OCR via OcrBackend (depends on image extraction from PPTX).

### Library
python-pptx (already in dev environment, needs to become a dependency).

### Scope
- New `presentation_processor.py` module
- Add `.pptx` to SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS
- Feed parsed text into existing chunk-embed-store pipeline
- Persist page_type and source_format in chunks
- Depends on schema columns existing (quarry-8si epic)
close_reason: PR #34 merged. PPTX ingestion complete.

title: Lux integration: Claude-native search results UI
description:
Lux integration: Claude-native search results UI.

Architecture: Option D hybrid — quarry-specific skill prompt + Lux data-explorer template.

The quarry plugin creates a skill (e.g. /quarry:browse or auto-activated on /find results) that composes Lux elements into an interactive search UI:

1. Results grid — group element with cards for each result: document title (text, bold), excerpt as markdown with search term highlights, relevance bar (progress element). Clickable cards via recv() to show full document context.

2. Collection sidebar — tree element showing databases and document counts with drill-down into per-document details. Combo dropdown for database selection. Adapts the data-explorer filter panel.

3. Filters panel — checkboxes for file type (PDF, code, markdown, spreadsheet), date range slider, source format combo. recv() captures filter changes, triggers re-search via quarry MCP tools, update() refreshes results.

4. Document preview — collapsing_header that expands to show full page content when a result card is clicked. For code files, syntax-highlighted markdown. For PDFs, page text with page number indicator.

5. Search analytics — plot element showing search frequency over time, table of popular queries. Adapts the dashboard Lux skill. Useful for /quarry status overview.

Delivery mechanism:
- Skill prompt in quarry plugin encodes the search UI layout as JSON templates
- Directly adapts the data-explorer Lux skill pattern
- Complements quarry-menubar: menubar is standalone macOS browsing, Lux surface is in-session agent-driven exploration
- MCP instructions provide formatting rules via Prior-Context Priming

title: PostToolUse hook matcher doesn't match standalone MCP tool names
description:
Quarry's PostToolUse suppress-output hook only fires when quarry is loaded as a plugin, not as a standalone MCP server.

## Root Cause

The matcher in hooks.json is:
```
"matcher": "mcp__plugin_quarry(-dev)?_quarry__.*"
```

This matches the plugin-namespaced tool names (`mcp__plugin_quarry_quarry__search_documents`) but NOT the standalone MCP server names (`mcp__quarry__search_documents`).

When quarry is loaded as a plugin (e.g., `--plugin-dir .` in the quarry repo), tools get the `mcp__plugin_quarry_quarry__` prefix — matcher hits. When quarry is loaded as a standalone MCP server (via .mcp.json or settings.json, e.g., in the tts repo), tools get the `mcp__quarry__` prefix — matcher misses.

## Observed Behavior

- In quarry repo: suppression works, tool output is formatted with unicode alignment
- In tts repo (or any other repo with quarry as standalone MCP): raw JSON output shown, no formatting

## Fix

Update the matcher to handle both patterns:
```json
"matcher": "mcp__(plugin_quarry(-dev)?_)?quarry__.*"
```

This matches:
- `mcp__quarry__*` (standalone)
- `mcp__plugin_quarry_quarry__*` (installed plugin)
- `mcp__plugin_quarry-dev_quarry__*` (dev plugin)

## Verification

Test in a session where quarry is a standalone MCP server (not plugin-dir). Run `/find` or any quarry tool — output should be formatted, not raw JSON.
close_reason: Closed

title: Bug: SessionStart hook doesn't invoke Python handler — no auto-registration or sync
description:
Z spec audit finding: handle_session_start() in hooks.py implements auto-registration and codebase sync, but session-start.sh never calls it. The Python handler is orphaned.

## Current State
- hooks.json registers SessionStart → session-start.sh
- session-start.sh deploys commands and sets permissions (correct)
- session-start.sh does NOT call 'quarry hooks session-start' 
- handle_session_start() in hooks.py is NEVER INVOKED
- Result: project directories are never auto-registered, codebase never synced at startup

## Fix
In session-start.sh, after command deployment and permission setup, add:
  quarry hooks session-start < /dev/stdin
Or register a second SessionStart hook in hooks.json that calls the Python handler directly.

## Z Spec Reference
docs/claude-code-quarry.tex, StartSessionWithSync operation.
The model proves codebaseIndexed=ztrue for all states after sync-enabled startup.
close_reason: Closed

title: Claude Code slash commands: /ingest, /find, /explain, /source, /quarry
description:
## Commands

/ingest <target> - Smart orchestrated ingest. Claude determines the right approach:
- Bare domain or sitemap URL -> find sitemap, call ingest_sitemap
- Single URL -> call ingest_url
- Directory path -> register_directory + sync_all_registrations
- File path -> call ingest_file
- No args -> ask what to ingest

/find <query> - Retrieve relevant passages with sources. Calls search_documents, presents results with document names, page numbers, and similarity. Offers to pull full page context via get_page.

/explain <topic> - Synthesize understanding from indexed docs. Searches, reads full pages for top results, produces a coherent explanation citing sources. Multi-step orchestration: search -> get_page -> synthesize.

/source <claim> - Provenance lookup. Where did I read about X? Finds the original document and page, shows the full passage in context. Focuses on exact location rather than breadth.

/quarry - Status dashboard and management:
- /quarry (no args) -> list_collections + status summary
- /quarry sync -> sync_all_registrations
- /quarry doctor -> runs quarry doctor via bash

Each command gets a prod variant (commands/find.md) and a dev variant (commands/find-dev.md) per dual-mode standard.

## Output Formatting (biff DES-001 pattern)

Adopt biff three-layer display architecture for all quarry MCP tool output.

### PostToolUse hook (suppress-output.sh)
Intercepts all quarry MCP tool responses. Splits into two channels:
- updatedMCPToolOutput: compact summary for the tool result panel
- additionalContext: full formatted output for the LLM to emit verbatim

### Silent commands (action confirmations, no LLM output needed)
- ingest_file: "31 chunks indexed from report.pdf"
- ingest_url: "31 chunks indexed from https://..."
- ingest_content: "1 chunk indexed from notes.md"
- delete_document: "Deleted smoke-test-inline.md (1 chunk)"
- delete_collection: "Deleted smoke-test (171 chunks)"
- register_directory, deregister_directory: confirmation line
- use_database: "Switched to work"

### Data commands (summary in panel, full output via additionalContext)
- search_documents: panel "3 results in smoke-test" / full formatted result list with doc name, page, snippet
- get_documents: panel "5 documents" / document table with names, chunks, timestamps
- list_collections: panel "4 collections" / collection table with counts
- get_page: panel "p.2 of report.pdf" / full page text
- ingest_sitemap: panel "5 ingested, 0 skipped" / progress log + per-URL results
- sync_all_registrations: panel "synced 3 directories" / per-directory results
- list_registrations: panel "3 registered" / directory table
- list_databases: panel "2 databases" / database table with sizes
- status: panel "default: 6 docs, 430 chunks" / full stats

### MCP server instructions field
Formatting guidance in the MCP server instructions (loads at initialize handshake, before any tool calls). This is critical - biff learned that skill prompt wording alone is insufficient; the instructions field provides prior-context delivery that makes verbatim formatting work.

### Formatter
Shared formatter for table output (adapted from biff DES-014). 80-column constrained tables for LLM consumption.

## References
- biff DESIGN.md DES-001 (three-layer display architecture, 12-16h of iteration)
- biff DESIGN.md DES-014 (column-constrained table formatter)
- biff hooks/suppress-output.sh as reference implementation
close_reason: PR #58 merged: slash commands, formatted output, PostToolUse hook

title: quarry install/uninstall — daemon lifecycle management
description:
Add 'quarry install' and 'quarry uninstall' commands that set up and tear down quarry as a system daemon.

quarry install detects the platform:
- macOS: writes ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.punt-labs.quarry.plist, runs launchctl load
- Linux: writes ~/.config/systemd/user/quarry.service, runs systemctl --user enable --now quarry

The service runs 'quarry serve --port 8420' (fixed default port, see quarry-cas).

quarry uninstall stops the daemon and removes the service file. Clean inverse.

The launchd plist should set KeepAlive=true (restart on crash). The systemd unit should set Restart=on-failure, RestartSec=5.

This is the Linux-side equivalent of 'brew services start quarry' on macOS. Users who install via Homebrew on macOS get daemon management via brew services instead (separate Homebrew formula work).
close_reason: Merged PR #97: quarry install/uninstall with daemon lifecycle

title: quarry install MCP registration fails during TestPyPI testing
description:
## Problem

`quarry install` registers the MCP server as `uvx --from punt-quarry quarry mcp`, which pulls from production PyPI. During the release workflow's TestPyPI testing phase (before approving to production), the MCP server fails to connect because `punt-quarry` doesn't exist on production PyPI yet.

This means the TestPyPI gate cannot fully validate the install flow — `quarry --help` passes but MCP registration is broken.

## Observed

```
quarry: uvx --from punt-quarry quarry mcp - ✗ Failed to connect
```

## Root Cause

`_MCP_COMMAND = "uvx"` and `_MCP_ARGS = ["--from", "punt-quarry", "quarry", "mcp"]` in `doctor.py` hardcode production PyPI resolution. No fallback to the already-installed binary on PATH.

## Possible Fixes

1. Register MCP using the local binary (`quarry mcp`) instead of `uvx` — works regardless of install source
2. Detect install source and adjust index URL for `uvx` accordingly
3. Accept the gap and only test MCP registration after production PyPI publish
close_reason: Obsolete: plugin architecture (quarry-h9k) replaced quarry install MCP registration. Plugin system handles MCP via .claude-plugin/plugin.json; SessionStart hook handles permissions. The old claude mcp add path is now legacy dead code.

title: infra: Add rename/move operation to database layer
description:
When a file is renamed or moved, the stored document_name and document_path in LanceDB become stale. Currently sync treats this as delete + re-ingest, wasting OCR and embedding compute.

## Database layer

Add rename_document() to database.py that updates document_name and document_path columns via table.update() with a WHERE filter. No re-embedding or re-chunking needed.

## Sync integration

In compute_sync_plan(), after building to_delete and to_ingest lists, cross-reference them: if a deletion candidate and an ingestion candidate share the same size+mtime, treat it as a rename instead of delete+ingest. mv preserves both attributes on the same filesystem.

SyncPlan gains a to_rename list of (old_record, new_path) tuples. sync_collection handles renames via rename_document() + registry update.

Ambiguous case (multiple files with identical size+mtime): fall back to delete+ingest.

## Three layers to update

1. LanceDB — document_name and document_path columns (rename_document)
2. SQLite registry — files table row: update path, document_name
3. Sync engine — rename detection in compute_sync_plan, rename execution in sync_collection
close_reason: Not pursuing rename support — delete+re-ingest is acceptable. Local OCR (ocr-1ke) reduces the cost of re-ingestion, making renames a non-issue.

title: tool: Search metadata filters
description:
Add optional filters to search_documents: page_type (content type) and source_format (file extension).

## Design Decisions (settled)

### Filter parameters
- `page_type` (string, optional) — coarse content filter. Values: text, code, spreadsheet, presentation, email. Example: `page_type="spreadsheet"` returns only tabular data chunks.
- `source_format` (string, optional) — precise format filter. Values: file extensions like `.pdf`, `.py`, `.xlsx`. Example: `source_format=".xlsx"` returns only Excel-sourced chunks.

### Implementation
Both filters become LanceDB SQL WHERE clauses on stored chunk columns. No post-filtering needed.

### Scope
- Add `page_type` and `source_format` parameters to `search_documents` MCP tool
- Add `--page-type` and `--source-format` CLI flags to `quarry search`
- Requires schema columns to exist first (depends on schema work in quarry-8si epic)

## Example use case
User searches: "DCF model", source_format=".xlsx" → returns only spreadsheet chunks containing DCF-related content, excluding PDF slides and markdown docs that mention DCF theory.

title: Fix hook import tax — lightweight entry point + stdlib extraction
description:
## Problem

Quarry's SessionStart hook (`session-sync.sh`) spawns a Python process that imports the entire quarry stack before running `handle_session_start` — which only needs `sqlite3` + `subprocess`.

**Measured cold start (installed binary, M2 MacBook Air): 1.47s**

The handler needs 0.12s of actual imports. The remaining 1.35s is wasted on:
- `pydantic_settings` (1.9s) via `quarry.config.Settings`
- `lancedb` (16.2s) via `quarry.database`
- `onnxruntime`, `pymupdf`, `bs4` via `quarry.pipeline`

These are pulled in by module-level imports in `quarry/hooks.py`, which hosts all hook handlers in one file. Every handler pays the import cost of every other handler.

## Root Cause

Three layers of the import tax anti-pattern (see punt-kit standards/hooks.md § 12):

1. **Eager `quarry/__init__.py`** — imports submodules at package load time
2. **Entry point routing through `__main__.py`** — `quarry hooks session-start` loads typer, the full CLI, and all commands before dispatching
3. **`hooks.py` module-level imports** — `from quarry.config import Settings, load_settings` triggers pydantic_settings; `from quarry.database import get_db` triggers lancedb; `from quarry.pipeline import ingest_content` triggers onnxruntime/pymupdf/bs4

## Solution

Apply the same three-layer fix shipped in biff v1.3.4 (DES-028):

### 1. Create `quarry/_stdlib.py`

Extract pure-stdlib functions needed by hook handlers:
- Registry path resolution (currently in `sync_registry.py` or `config.py`)
- Background sync subprocess spawn logic
- Any `find_git_root` / `is_enabled` equivalents

**Invariant**: every function uses only stdlib imports. Docstring must state this.

### 2. Create `quarry-hook` console script entry point

New file: `src/quarry/_hook_entry.py`
- Register in pyproject.toml: `quarry-hook = "quarry._hook_entry:main"`
- Parse `sys.argv` directly, dispatch to handler functions
- Bypass `__main__.py`, typer, and the CLI framework entirely

Update all hook shell scripts: `quarry hooks` → `quarry-hook`

### 3. Make `quarry/__init__.py` lazy via PEP 562

Replace eager imports with `__getattr__` that resolves on first access and caches in `globals()`. Use `TYPE_CHECKING` block for static analysis.

## Expected Result

Cold start: **1.47s → ~0.3s** (matching lux's lightweight level)

## Reference Implementation

- `biff/src/biff/_stdlib.py` — stdlib-only module pattern
- `biff/src/biff/_hook_entry.py` — lightweight entry point
- `biff/src/biff/__init__.py` — PEP 562 lazy loading
- `biff/DESIGN.md` DES-028 — full ADR with alternatives rejected
- `punt-kit/standards/hooks.md` § 12 — the standard

## Verification

1. `time quarry-hook claude-code session-start` on installed binary < 0.5s cold
2. All existing tests pass
3. `_stdlib.py` has zero third-party imports (grep to verify)
4. Quality gates clean (ruff, mypy, pyright, pytest)
close_reason: Merged PR #94: three-layer hook import tax fix (1.48s → 0.24s cold start)

title: /capture command: capture conversation research as indexed documents
description:
A new quarry slash command that captures recent conversation discussion as a structured research document and indexes it for future semantic search.

## What it does

`/stash [topic]` or `/quarry:stash [topic]` takes the recent conversation context and:

1. Generates a structured research markdown document (title, date, context, findings, sources)
2. Writes it to a configurable location (e.g., `./research/` in the current project)
3. Ingests it into the quarry collection for immediate semantic search

The command distills ephemeral conversation knowledge into a persistent, searchable artifact — bridging the gap between 'we discussed this' and 'we can find this later.'

## Motivation

During a tts session, we researched ElevenLabs WebSocket APIs, investigated JSONL streaming behavior, and reached conclusions about feasibility. The research was valuable but trapped in conversation context that would be lost on session end. Manually writing it to `research/research-2026-02-27-elevenlabs-realtime-apis.md` and ingesting into quarry preserved it — but the process was manual. /stash automates this pattern.

## UX

```
> /stash ElevenLabs WebSocket APIs

Captured research: ElevenLabs WebSocket APIs
  Written to: ./research/research-2026-02-27-elevenlabs-websocket-apis.md
  Indexed in: punt-labs (12 chunks)
```

With no argument, the command infers the topic from recent conversation context.

## Design considerations

- **Output location:** Check for `./research/` directory first. If it doesn't exist, use a configurable default (quarry config or .quarry.toml). Fallback: current directory.
- **Filename convention:** `research-{date}-{slugified-topic}.md` (matches existing convention in tts/research/)
- **Document structure:** YAML frontmatter (title, date, tags) + markdown body with sections: Context, Findings, Key Data, Sources.
- **Ingestion:** After writing the file, call quarry ingest to index it immediately. Use the project's registered collection if one exists, otherwise the default collection.
- **Idempotency:** If a file with the same slug already exists, append or ask before overwriting.
- **Scope:** The command generates the document content via the LLM — the skill prompt instructs Claude to summarize recent discussion into research format. Quarry's role is the ingestion and the command scaffold.

## Integration

This is a quarry skill/command. It uses:
- The LLM (via skill prompt) to generate the research document from conversation context
- The Write tool to create the file
- The quarry ingest_file MCP tool to index it immediately

No new MCP tools needed — it composes existing capabilities (LLM summarization + file write + quarry ingest).

title: Reconsider MCP tool names for clarity
description:
Reconsider MCP tool names for clarity. Joint with quarry-6x0 (unified ingest).

## Analysis (2026-02-28)

### Current State: 19 MCP tools, 17 CLI commands, 5 slash commands

Problems:
1. **Ingest sprawl** — 5 MCP tools (ingest_file, ingest_url, ingest_content, ingest_sitemap, ingest_auto), 3 CLI commands (ingest-file, ingest-url, ingest-sitemap), 1 slash command (/ingest). The slash command already does it right.
2. **Name disagreements** — search_documents vs search vs /find. get_documents vs list. Verbose MCP names don't match terse CLI names.
3. **Gaps** — status, use, page, ingest_content have no CLI equivalent.

### Proposed Unified Naming

One name per capability, shared across CLI and MCP. Slash commands may use friendlier names for prompt-driven variants.

**Product commands (Layer 1):**

| Name         | CLI                          | MCP Tool       | What it does                         |
|--------------|------------------------------|----------------|--------------------------------------|
| search       | quarry search <query>        | search         | Semantic search                      |
| ingest       | quarry ingest <path-or-url>  | ingest         | Ingest anything — detect from input  |
| page         | quarry page <doc> <n>        | page           | Get full text of a page              |
| sync         | quarry sync                  | sync           | Sync all registered directories      |
| documents    | quarry documents             | documents      | List indexed documents               |
| collections  | quarry collections           | collections    | List collections                     |
| databases    | quarry databases             | databases      | List databases                       |
| use          | quarry use <name>            | use            | Switch active database               |
| register     | quarry register <dir>        | register       | Register directory for sync          |
| deregister   | quarry deregister <dir>      | deregister     | Remove directory registration        |
| registrations| quarry registrations         | registrations  | List registered directories          |
| delete       | quarry delete <doc-or-coll>  | delete         | Delete document or collection        |

**Admin commands (Layer 2):**

version, doctor, status, install, serve, mcp

**Hook dispatcher (Layer 3, hidden):**

quarry hook session-start, quarry hook post-web-fetch, quarry hook pre-compact

**Slash commands (prompt-driven, call search MCP tool):**

/find — search + Claude interprets results
/explain — search + Claude explains topic
/source — search + Claude finds origin document
/ingest — maps to ingest MCP tool
/quarry — management (status, sync, collections, databases, registrations)

### Key Decisions

1. **Unified ingest** — one MCP tool with path, url, or content params. Auto-detects sitemap from URL. Replaces 5 tools with 1.
2. **Unified delete** — one command, detect document vs collection from argument (or --collection flag).
3. **search not find** — CLI/MCP use "search" (deterministic). /find is the slash command (prompt-driven).
4. **Drop verbose prefixes** — search_documents → search, get_documents → documents, list_collections → collections, register_directory → register.

### Search Funnel (user POV)

ingest → documents/collections → search → page

CLI handles all deterministic operations. Slash commands add LLM interpretation on top of search. /explain and /source don't need CLI equivalents — they're prompt behaviors.
close_reason: Analysis complete. Implementation tracked in quarry-7t0 (rename MCP tools). Full naming analysis preserved in this bead's description.
